title: Mega Charizard X Battle Guide
summary: A [Fire]/[Dragon] physical setup [sweeper] whose [tough-claws] turns every contact move into a 1.3x nuke after a [sing]le [dragon-dance].
strengths: [tough-claws] plus 130 Atk produces the best contact-move power in the tier
[Fire]/[Dragon] STAB cove[rage] is hard to wall
One [dragon-dance] outruns the entire unboosted metagame
weaknesses: [Rock]-type priority users shut down setup attempts
Lacks finishing power without a [dragon-dance] first

recommendedMoves.reason: Atk and Speed boost that seals the sweep win condition

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Charizard X is a [Fire]/[Dragon] physical setup [sweeper] built a[round] [tough-claws], which multiplies every contact move by 1.3x. Combined with 130 Atk and [dragon-dance], it requires only a [sing]le setup turn to outrun the entire unboosted metagame and start cleaning teams. Where the base form is a special [solar-power] nuke, Mega X is its exact physical mirror — a dedicated late-game finisher.

## Strengths
[tough-claws]-boosted [flare-blitz] and [dragon-claw] put out top-tier physical damage, and the [Fire]/[Dragon] STAB combo is uniquely difficult to resist — very few common pivots wall both. A [sing]le [dragon-dance] pushes effective Speed past 150, so it simply wins every unboosted Speed race. Post-Mega, base 111 Defense and 78 HP combine to survive one weak physical attack, giving Mega X the breathing room to actually set up a Dance.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Rock], [Ground], and [Dragon] all hit for 2x, and [stone-edge] users in particular punish any attempt to [dragon-dance] in their face. Priority attackers threaten the setup turn, especially if the team has no screens. [flare-blitz] re[coil] (1/3 max HP on miss) makes long matches dangerous, and the pre-Mega turn is fragile enough that a predicted super-effective hit can remove Charizard X before it ever sets up.

## Recommended Moveset
[dragon-claw] is the locked STAB because it deals clean, re[coil]-free damage every turn and is still [tough-claws]-boosted. [flare-blitz] is the physical [Fire] STAB finisher — with [tough-claws], STAB, and Adamant stacked, it hits one of the highest damage numbers in the format. [earthquake] is mandatory cove[rage] for [Rock], [Steel], and [Electric] walls. The final slot is [dragon-dance] — without it, Mega X lacks a real win condition against offense.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[charizardite-x] is the only item — no alternative exists. [tough-claws] is locked in automatically on Mega Evolution. For Tera, [Fire] deepens [flare-blitz] damage while keeping STAB cove[rage] intact, and [Dragon] pushes [dragon-claw] into the upper damage tier. A defensive Tera [Fighting] is also seen at the top level because it removes the [Rock] weakness, though it trades offensive ceiling for safety.

## Teammates & Synergy
A [stealth-rock] remover is effectively mandatory — the entry cost is too high otherwise. [fake-out] or slow pivot support helps buy the [dragon-dance] turn safely, and a dedicated late-game cleaner is useful for cleanup after Mega X has softened the board. A [Dark]-type partner that can pressure Fairies in advance is ideal, since Fairies are the main remaining check.

## Play Patterns
Enter only after [stealth-rock] is removed, and c[lick] [dragon-dance] the moment a non-priority defensive mon switches in. Lead with [dragon-claw] for sustainable damage and save [flare-blitz] for kill-confirmed situations only — the re[coil] clock is a real constraint.
